title: anthyllis vulneraria proles pulchella
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria proles pulchella, also known as kidney vetch or common kidney vetch, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin irritations, and urinary tract issues due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, including kidney problems and digestive issues.
